task_completion.description: Verify that the agent successfully logged into Rocket.Chat.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rocket.Chat main interface is visible (showing channels, workspace, or user profile), indicating the login screen was successfully bypassed using the provided credentials.

task_completion.description: Verify navigation to the Administration or Workspace settings panel.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rocket.Chat Administration or Workspace settings sidebar/menu is open and visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify navigation to the OAuth Apps configuration section.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'OAuth Apps' or 'OAuth' page/list is displayed within the Administration settings area.

task_completion.description: Verify the new OAuth Application Name is entered correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A form for creating a new OAuth application is visible, and the text 'Release Dashboard' is entered into the 'Application Name' field.

task_completion.description: Verify the Redirect URI is entered correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'http://dashboard.internal/callback' is entered into the 'Redirect URI' field on the OAuth application form.

task_completion.description: Verify the application is saved and set as active.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The application 'Release Dashboard' appears in the saved list of OAuth Apps, or a success screen showing the generated Client ID/Secret is visible. The 'Active' status toggle must be enabled (usually default) or indicated as true.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used a web browser to interact with Rocket.Chat.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Rocket.Chat interface is rendered within a standard web browser window (e.g., Chromium, Firefox), not via terminal commands or API testing tools like Postman.

integrity.description: Verify the interaction occurred within the genuine Rocket.Chat application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The UI matches Rocket.Chat's standard layout (sidebar, admin panel, forms) and is not a text editor, notes app, or generic mock HTML page.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use terminal commands to bypass the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows showing curl, wget, or database commands (like MongoDB queries) used to directly inject the OAuth application into the backend.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target OAuth Application name must be exactly 'Release Dashboard'. The target Redirect URI must be exactly 'http://dashboard.internal/callback'.
